PARK FOREST CARE CENTER LLC

7045 STUART ST, WESTMINSTER, CO 80030

Setting
Urban
2 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 2/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.90 2.87 3.89 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.49 0.50 0.66 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.49 0.64 0.66 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.92 1.73 2.58 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.98 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.76
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.39
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.71
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
63.5%
annual
RN turnover
45.5%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.02
near national average
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.74
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-06-03 7 7 0 40 1 40
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-11-30 10 7 3 243 1 243
Total weighted health score
90.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (28)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

2 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
24 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0923 Have enough outside ventilation via a window or mechanical ventilation, or both. E 2025-06-03 2025-06-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. L 2024-12-31 2025-01-01
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-12-31 2025-01-01
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2024-12-31 2025-01-01
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. C 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. C 2023-11-30 2023-12-22
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. E 2022-08-23 2022-11-09
0644 Coordinate assessments with the pre-admission screening and resident review program; and referring for services as needed. D 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. E 2022-08-23 2022-11-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0791 Provide or obtain dental services for each resident. D 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2022-08-23 2022-09-13
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2022-08-23 2022-09-17
